name: ChristianCyclingdotorg Group Ride
description:
Welcome to the Christian Cycling.org group ride.  

Christian cycling.org is a group of people who share the love of Christ and cycling. We work together to encourage each other while we ride to bring out the best in everyone.  

All are welcome to join us no matter where you are in their life, cycling or fitness. 

Join us for this banded group social ride, meaning you will not be dropped as long as you are peddling. 

This ride has a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature should teleport you back into the main group.

We love to chat on our rides so join us on discord
<https://discord.gg/7gJBR84UU3>  Alpha channel

Website www.christiancycling.org
Zwift club christiancyclclingdotorg
Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/ChristianCycling

name: Oxygenaddict Triathlon Power Hour
description: This session is brought to you by Team OxygenAddict Triathlon Head Coach Rob Wilby. The session is designed to increase your Functional Threshold Power, improve your triathlon specific time-trialling ability to boost your triathlon bike leg over every distance!

name: ZSUN Ladies Social
description:
A ladies-only group ride hosted by ZSUN, designed to offer a welcoming and supportive space for female Zwifters of all abilities.

Follow the beacon, enjoy the chat, and become part of a growing community of women who ride together every week.

🗓️ Tuesday | ~60 mins | Social pace (1.8–2.2 w/kg)
🚺 Women-only
💬 Friendly group dynamics
🎁 Unlock the ZSUN kit

Ride with confidence, strength, and sisterhood – all are welcome.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Ladies Social (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
This is a ladies only social event hosted by ZSUN Ladies

All sub group will ride the ZSUN Jerseys.

Please follow the ride leader's instructions.

Join us on facebook groups: ZSUN https://www.facebook.com/groups/985532344865361/

Join the ZSUN Discord Server!

<https://discord.gg/HTccggd?fbclid=IwAR2dCv2o4oZLIfQxiBH0lwuUmXIhHrsC6DEd3YlBQn2iJtF08seRDKxOxKI>
Check out the ZSUN community on Discord - hang out with 69 other members and enjoy free voice and text chat.

discord.gg

Please use Push to Talk to avoid echo feedback.

#zsun

name: JETT Warm up Ride for JZMR (2.5~3.0w/kg)
description:
JETT Warming up Ride for JZMR
Power ave. 2.5w/kg to 3.0w/kg
JZMR=JETT Zwift Morning Race will start 15 minutes after the end of this event. Of course, all ZWIFTers are welcome to participate. Leaders and front riders will lead the group with 2.5 to 3.0w/kg. Riders who have a hard time with the above power can try grouping by making good use of drafting.
If you are far behind the group, please say "HELP! at the ZWIFT CAHT and Sweeper will come to your aid. Unless you say "HELP! Sweeper will not go to your aid unless you say "HELP! If at all possible, please do so within 10 seconds of each other.
Rules for this group ride
1)Keep the grouping in mind at all times (this is not a race).
2)Do not go further than the leader's yellow beacon (20m or more)
3)TT Bikes can be used, but they do not provide a drafting effect.
4)Follow the leader's instruction for sprint.
30 minute Group Ride, follow the leader's instructions, and work together to make it a great group and a great workout!

Please join to our Facebook page.
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftjett/

----
JETT Warming up Ride for JZMR
パワー ave.2.5w/kg〜3.0w/kg
JZMR=JETT Zwift Morning Raceはこのイベント終了から15分後に始まります。もちろん、ZWIFTer誰でも参加できます。 リーダーおよび先頭は2.5〜3.0w/kgでグループを引っ張ります。上記のパワーでは辛いというライダーはドラフティングをうまく活用してグルーピングにトライして下さい。
もし、集団から後方に離れてしまった場合、「HELP!!」とZWIFT CAHTで申し出てください。Sweeperが支援に向かいます。「HELP!!」の申し出がない限り、Sweeperは支援には行きません。また、可能な限りタイム差１０秒以内に申し出をお願いします。
このグループライドのルール
・常にグルーピングを心がけること(レースではありません）
・リーダーの黄色いビーコンよりも前に出ないこと（２０m以上）
・TT Bikeは使用可能ですが、ドラフティング効果を得られません。
・スプリントはリーダー指示に従ってください。
30分のGroup Ride、リーダー指示に従い、みんなで協力して最高のグループ、最高のワークアウトにしましょう！

皆さんをFacebookページでお待ちしております！
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftjett/

#JETT

name: Turn It Up Tuesdays
description:
Welcome to Turn It Up Tuesdays. Whether you’re a new runner getting into fitness, an experienced runner who wants to compete for a new personal best, or an athlete craving a great workout alongside an awesome crew of fellow athletes, these weekly structured workouts promise up-tempo runs that’ll boost you up and help you achieve your running goals. 

#turnituptuesday #tiut

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: The Velos Klick - 1km Challenge
description: A klick is a military term for a distance of 1km, and so Team Velos brings you a challenge to complete 1km in the fastest time possible. We will change courses monthly (all Flat courses) and we will have winners from each category at the end of the month. Choose your bike very carefully as it could make all the difference, the challenge is available every night during the week use it as a warm up for a race or just use it to improve your 1km time or power figures, whatever just have fun.

name: Madhare Flat and Fast
description:
Join our Madhare sessions every Tuesday & Saturday as part of our weekly Autumn/Winter training for new and experienced Zwifters.
Tuesday's
Each week we'll explore another route from the worlds of Zwift that's flat and fast!
Distances will range between 20-50km with the sole intention of ABSOLUTELY SMASHING IT around the flats! (pace dependent on CAT)
Get into a rhythm and see your PB improve with every week you ride
Also perfect if you just want to get on your trainer and join a social pedal
Our beacon is there to set a pace and to talk all things cycling so jump on and join in!
It's not a race, however, if you want to zip off ahead and get that PB on the sprint, or drop back and relax you can do that too!
Join the Mad Hare official Zwift club! Email or follow one of our leaders for an invite.

eventSubgroups.name: Madhare Flat and Fast (C)
eventSubgroups.description:
Join our Madhare sessions Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday as part of our weekly Autumn/Winter training for new and experienced Zwifters.
Each time we'll explore another route from the worlds of Zwift; from fast and flat laps to challenging climbs, and everything in between!
Our beacon is there to set a pace and to talk all things cycling so jump on and join in!
It's not a race, however, if you want to zip off ahead and get that PB on the sprint, or drop back and relax you can do that too!
*Cat C - 1 - 5w/kg*
Join the Mad Hare official Zwift club! Email  or follow one of our leaders for an invite.

name: GGCC Glasgow Green CC Tuesday Training ride
description:
GGCC group rides are TRAINING events, category C, with a yellow beacon leader. The beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average wherever possible.

Please look out for our training rides 3 days a week, every week - Tuesdays at 20.45 UTC, Thursdays at 21.00 UTC and Saturdays at 11.30am UTC. Use #ggcc to search, as in https://zwift.com/events/tag/ggcc

The Glasgow Green Cycle Club (GGCC) training rides are intended to provide a challenge for riders, and are open to all timezones. You should ride as a group, following the instructions of the beacon ride leader(s) for any sprints, intervals and spicy segments, regrouping after each when requested. All our group rides also have a closing minirace, to start from behind the beacon.

GGCC is a Glasgow based cycling club whose “in real life” (irl) club runs and training rides are always organized into pace groups, which match the Zwift categories quite well. GGCC also has a good race pedigree and presence in Scotland, and so we have organized this training ride series to suit what we believe is relevant to bike riders’ interests in performance and improvement in our club, in other Glasgow clubs, in Scotland and the UK. Racers will, no doubt, aspire to try our other events, both our Monday 20.10 UTC Zwift races, and our Friday 19.45 UTC TTs.

As we expect our club’s riders to be able to ride 50 miles at a 15mph average on our Sunday rides, such steady riding members will be comfortable with Zwift Category C, and in our various rides we cater for all abilities, through the Zwift ABCD levels. Beacon leaders communicate via Zwift messaging (always) and/or Discord (sometimes) to vary the pace to allow for sprints, intervals, and optionally a mini-race towards the end of the rides, with regrouping after each segment. Make as much use of drafting as you can, for more effective sprinting and intervals.

Results can be seen at the end of the ride on screen, and we will, in common with many other ride series, publish your results on zwiftpower.com too, based on each category.

All bikes and PUs are allowed, but better not to use TT bikes (which don’t draft) on our group rides and races. Please add GGCC (X) to your Zwift name to allow your ride presence to be recognized, where X is your desired ride category. Categories are related to your Functional Threshold Power (FTP); use your FTP and then divide by your weight in kg, then pick the correct ride and group.

C: 2 W/kg to 3 W/kg FTP; the beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average.

PLEASE NOTE: Strava data for our rides must be open (not private or hidden). Please use a heart monitor so that we can monitor correlated power and heart rate to help assure fair riding.
You can use our Discord channel to talk to each other at https://discord.gg/UDhdEQA
Please see the GGCC Zwift page link at http://www.glasgowgreencycleclub.co.uk/ for more.

For more about specific Zwift courses for our events, see the Zwift Companion schedule, and also https://zwiftinsider.com/ where there is a wealth of information about all things Zwift.

#ggcc

name: Rhino Racing Short Crits
description:
Fast and fun criterium races with five race times every Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday.
A power meter or smart trainer is required for entry. A ZwiftPower account is required to be counted in the official results.
View the calendar of all Rhino Racing events: https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/rhinoracing
Do you enjoy these events? We want to hear from you. Share your feedback on the Rhino Racing Discord.
Interested in team racing? We do ZRL, Dirt Racing Series, Club Ladder, and WTRL TTT events. Join us! https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (A)
NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (B)

name: Team VTO Multisport Social Ride
description:
Team VTO presents our weekly social ride where Multisport & Paratriathletes from around the world 🌍 meet for a 1 hour social ride. 🏊🏼‍♂️🚴🏼‍♂️🏃🏻‍♂️🇦🇺.

We will ride at around 2.0-2.5 W/kg but will adjust the pace as necessary for the bunch. Please stay together as a group around the Beacon. It is a group ride - not a race!
**The last 20 mins (Lap 3) will feature an optional HOT LAP or INTERVAL TRAINING SESSION.

- Latest World News on Multisports 📰
- Special guest-star leaders and Pro's ⭐️
- In-Game Rides & Quizzes 🤨

Join our Zwift Club - Team Zwift Tri Oz (on your companion app 👉🏻📲

To be involved in our community (+ claim your prize if applicable), join our FB group;
www.facebook.com/groups/thenewzwifttrioz/

name: Dowsett's TT Tips
description: It's back!  A no drop group training session for the TT'ing kind amongst us who love a challenge.  Tune into Youtube to watch and listen to Alex do the session live with you.  All sessions will be aimed at helping you improve your TT primarily, but there's plenty of crossover whereby you'll improve your FTP which helps every aspect of your racing.  Expect a variety of sessions, from Over Unders, Race Winning Intervals, Sweetspot and V02 Max work and more.

name: Aussie Crit Crushers 1/2
description:
Aussie Crit Crushers Race is a fun short sharp hitout that consists of two events back to back. When signing up for Race #1 make sure to register for race #2.

Race #1 is on Crit City -Downtown Dolphin ( x4 laps)
Race #2 is on New York -LaGuardia Loop ( x4 laps)

Jump in race both events to be honored winning the XP Cup!
This is a graded Crit race, yes you can crush your mates, whilst also crushing your own PR times each week! 
Com’on what are you waiting for SIGN UP your mates are waiting for you to join them!

name: TPR Pelo x Attaquer - Maeve Plouffe
description:
For this week's ride we are partnering with the Attaquer crew who will be joining TPR Pelo ride! There will be a sweet Attaquer prize pack up for grabs during the ride so make sure you bunch up to the beacon and stay close to Jethro (hint, hint)

The Pelo is an easy 60-minute weekly ride where we can talk a bit about our favourite Aussie cyclists and how they are going over in Europe and start some discussions as to what we think will happen in the upcoming races each week. The ride will automatically play the latest episode of The Press Room Podcast presented by Zwift so, if you prefer to simply listen and pedal then you are more than welcome to tag along with us and be apart of the community!

The Press Room Podcast is a cycling podcast featuring extended interviews with some of the world's best cyclists, key staff personnel and other notable cycling personalities. Through thought provoking and casual "on the couch" interviews, The Press Room pulls back the curtains on professional cyclists to highlight the stories, relationships and characters that are often hidden beyond the results sheet.

The Press Room Podcast presented by Zwift is available for download on all podcast players such as Spotify, Apple and Google podcasts. Be sure to subscribe to the pod and follow the Instagram and Youtube channel for all the latest updates.

Join The Press Room Pelo CC Zwift Club!

Episode 8 of TPR features Australia's most exciting prospect on the track and on the road - Maeve Plouffe. Maeve runs us through her recent success at the Track Nationals (despite some challenges) where she won back the IP title, her World Tour ambitions on the road for 2023 and we discuss how she got into the sport before diving deep in to the art of sprinting and who inspires her as a female athlete.

The Press Room podcast is presented by our title sponsor Zwift.

eventSubgroups.name: TPR Pelo x Attaquer - Maeve Plouffe (D)

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Stage 2: Zwift Games: Hudson Hustle
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

(profile.playerType == "BROADCASTER") ||

(subgroup.label == 4 && scoring.current < 270) ||

name: JETT ZWIFT Morning Race
description:
Welcome to Japan Zwift morning race! Let's enjoy this race !! All are welcome. 
Do your best about 1hour race.We hope that Participants gets a great workout and has some good fun in this Race.
We are rooting for your better training race.

GROUPS:
Use your FTP and then divide by your weight in kg then pick the correct group:
A:4.0 W/kg FTP or higer
B:3.2 W/kg to 4.0W/kg FTP
C:2.5 W/kg to 3.1W/kg FTP
D:Under2.4 W/kg FTP

We hope that Participants gets a great workout and has some good fun in this Race.
Ride On!! 

#jett #japan

eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to Japan Zwift morning race! Let's enjoy this race !! All are welcome.

name: Stage 2: Zwift Games: Hudson Hustle | Advanced
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

eventSubgroups.name: Stage 2: Zwift Games: Hudson Hustle | Advanced (A)

name: Aussie Crit Crushers 2/2
description:
Aussie Crit Crushers Race is a fun short sharp hitout that consists of two events back to back. When signing up for Race #1 make sure to register for race #2.

Race #1 is on Crit City -Downtown Dolphin x4 laps 
Race #2 is on New York -LaGuardia Loop x 4 laps

Jump in race both events to be honored with winning the XP Cup!
This is a graded Crit race, yes you can crush your mates, whilst also crushing your own PR times each week! 
Com’on what are you waiting for SIGN UP, your mates are waiting for you to join them!

#zwiftrace

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Ride to End ALZ
description:
The Ride to End ALZ South Carolina is a three-day, cross-state journey for cycling enthusiasts. Now in its 18th year, the unique Group Ride travels from beautiful Upstate South Carolina to the picturesque Charleston coast July 10-12, 2026. Riders may choose between two mileage options: The Grand Tour (257 miles) or The Half Tour (136 miles). This event is known for its incredible support along the route and camaraderie that only a multi-day ride can bring. Participants may also ride at their own pace from anywhere in our month-long Virtual Challenge. Either way, funds raised by riders benefit the Alzheimer's Association and fuel cutting-edge research targeted to change the trajectory of Alzheimer’s disease.
Get all the details at alz.org/ridesc

This Zwift ride is open to everyone and all paces, and our goal is to fill you in on the details of the incredible impact Ride to End ALZ has on making progress towards the research and treatment of Alzheimers through funding with the Alzheimer’s Association. In addition to the 3-day Group Ride in SC, there is also a month-long Virtual Challenge option! Join us, unlock the Ride to End ALZ kit, and learn more about how YOU can make a difference in the fight against Alzheimer’s! Join our Facebook group to get all the latest info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/alzRTR
More details at alz.org/ridesc

name: DIRT Family Values Ride
description:
Welcome to the DIRT Family Values Ride

Please join us weekly on Tuesday evenings for a 1.5 – 1.8 w/kg 50 minute group ride. 

Our yellow beacon group leader will remain in the 1.5 - 1.8 w/kg range for the entirety of the ride. This is a social ride.  All are welcome to join. Please follow the yellow beacon at all times.

Team DIRT is an acronym for Dads Inside Riding Trainers. We are a brotherhood like-minded moms and dads who love to make time for Zwift around work and family. We are a social racing team consisting mostly of mid-level Zwifters. The Family Values Ride idea came about because a lot of us have work and family obligations during the week and this would be a perfect way to squeeze in a great ride.

Don't forget to add "DIRT" after your name and make sure you are not on a TT bike.
 
Please join us on Discord at https://discord.gg/sUnwqPZ
 
And, Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZWIFTDIRT
 
Also, find us at IndoorSpecialist.com

name: Faster Masters B & C Group Ride
description:
Tuesday’s ride includes B and C Category Groups (teams). Ride concept is two-fold: Group B departs first. Group C starts one minute later. Group B will maintain a steady pace unless directed otherwise by the yellow beacon (team leader). Group C will also maintain a steady pace unless directed otherwise by the yellow beacon. This ride offers C level riders the opportunity to train at a higher level if they wish by starting with Group B for a period of time, then fall back with Group C to complete the ride. Doing so week after week riders can try to stay in the faster group longer each week until they are able to remain with Group B for the entire event. Start times: Group B, 6:00 PM, ET (New York Time); Group C beginning at 6:01 PM. The pace efforts for Group C will be from 2.5 to 3.1 w/kg; Group B’s pace effort is 3.2 to 4.0 w/kg. This ride setup is also a good format should Freebirds (flyers) join Group C; the yellow beacon can advise Freebirds to either return to the group or attack trying to catch Group B ahead. If utilized, this should be done early in the ride. 

Both groups include a 30-minute Late Join period. During this time, dropped riders can use the Late Join feature to rejoin. If you find that the group you selected is either too fast or too slow for your pace, you can use Late Join to switch groups within the first 30 minutes of the start time of the group you would like to join. 

We do not sweep during the first 30 minutes of a ride. If you fall behind for any reason during this time, we recommend using Late Join to re-join near the yellow beacon position. We usually do not have designated sweepers (Red Beacon). After the first 30 minutes, if you get dropped and want to rejoin your group, please send a message in the format HELP(X), where X is the number of seconds you are off the back (for example, HELP5). If you reach out early, one or more stronger riders may come to assist you. If a sweep is needed we recommend you message HELP within 10 seconds.

Connect with Faster Masters on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/2286721591625605

name: Ascenders Asia Morning Group Run
description:
Come and join the Ascenders as we put on our running shoes and run
daily with our friends too by starting our day in a healthy way. Research
shows that just 30 minutes of running can have huge benefits on your
short-term and long-term health. So we designed our running events to
motivate athletes to run 30 minutes each day to boost your metabolism and
use those calories. Running will also help you look good and feel stronger.

Our group run is designed with runners of all levels in mind and set the
pace range to be determined by our Zwifter Runners. So find a group pace
that matches how you feel each day and make those steps count for you.
Runners who went beyond the event duration will find that we have selected
courses that will help you earn the Route Badges when you run the full
distance.

So lace up those running shoes as everybody is welcome to join with our
friendly and motivating runners.

# <https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/> Join Ascenders on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# <https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/> Ascenders Discord Channel:
https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Triathletes on Zwift Tuesday High Intensity Workout
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

This workout is part of a 24-week progressive series that builds from All Out to Super Threshold efforts over subsequent weeks.  The All-Out efforts aid to develop rider's top end aerobic abilities to push big wattage for relatively short periods of time with controlled rest periods.  The All Out and Super/Sub Threshold efforts allow riders to focus on improving their ability to ride at or slightly above their FTP for extended periods of time, again with fairly moderate rest intervals.  Workouts begin with a warm up period  where riders will go through short progressive builds towards the workouts goal FTP percentage.  A main set of intervals will follow that focuse on developing aerobic capacity.  Each of the 24-week workouts builds off the preceding workout in terms of duration of main set intervals.

WORKOUT DESIGNER

AJ Baucco is a well established professional triathlete from Cleveland, Ohio. He founded AJ Baucco Coaching, which has coached over 150 triathletes in the last 5 years. He also manages 3 different age group triathlon teams; The Baucco Squad, TRS Racing, and Team INFINIT Performance. Over the years, AJ Baucco has become a premier triathlon coach. He has helped athletes achieve personal bests, stand on podiums, and even race world championship events. On the race course, AJ Baucco has achieved top results at every distance from super sprint to Ironman. Most recently, he was on the winning team for 6 different Major League Triathlon events, and 2 back to back series championships.

Contact – 
Websites – www.baucco.com, www.ajbcoaching.com, www.bauccosquad.com
Twitter - @irunshirtless
Instagram - @ajbaucco

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins.  Everyone stays together regardless of power output.   That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other.  Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level.   

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

name: PACK🐺 SUB2 Weekly Grind
description:
This PACK SUB2 event is aimed at Zwifters looking for a friendly group ride experience with a pace that matches the description of less than 2w/kg. It’s a great introduction to group riding with full pace instructions via text and voice chat using discord. You can sign up early via the web or mobile app and late join is enabled.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

name: Giant Tempo Tuesday
description:
Building up fitness into a new Cat? Looking to join a fun social ride?
Our ride leaders will do their best to keep the group together during a 60min ride at a steady pace. 

Cat C is aiming for an introductory 2.3-2.6w/kg while allowing sprints.
Looking for more? Cat B group starts in a delayed chase format in hopes to catch within the hour. Ride leaders will do their best to keep the group together at a low 3w/kg pack pace.

Whichever you pick, come hang out with Team Giant for laughs, and motivation!

name: Rhino Racing Short Crits
description:
Fast and fun criterium races with five race times every Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday.
A power meter or smart trainer is required for entry. A ZwiftPower account is required to be counted in the official results.
View the calendar of all Rhino Racing events: https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/rhinoracing
Do you enjoy these events? We want to hear from you. Share your feedback on the Rhino Racing Discord.
Interested in team racing? We do ZRL, Dirt Racing Series, Club Ladder, and WTRL TTT events. Join us! https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: Booty Thompkins
description:
The SweatFest series will be back next fall.  In it's place we have our "one off" non-series races. If you have a specific course you'd like to see added let us know on our FB page. Link below. 

Day and Time: Every Tuesday 3:30pm & 6:30pm Pacific
We use the standard ZRS category system.

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/132629184105216

name: EZR Tempo Tuesday
description:
EZR Tempo Tuesday

All riders are welcome to join EZ Riders (EZR) for a nice Tempo Ride every Tuesday evening. The ride leader will be staying within the advertised average pace as we explore the wonderful world of Zwift. Our focus is usually on flatter routes so we can all ride and stay together at a steady pace.  

Ride leaders may choose to activate the fence for this ride as a reminder to ride together. This ride could be a challenge for some and a recovery for others, so please respect the pace.  

We are a fun and supportive group of friends and hope you can join us.

Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZER.Cycling 

#zer #ezr #ezriders

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: Phoenix Rising United
description:
The Phoenix Rising United ride is designed to leave the stresses of the day behind us. We're here for you to help you turn your day around, or to make a great day shine even brighter. We are here for your recovery, warm up/cool down, or threshold workout. Whatever your reasons are, we've got you covered. Our average pace is 26-30kph (16-19mph) with power target average of 1.4 to 1.7 W/kg, and our leaders will not exceed those paces regardless of the route. The leader has the option to activate the no-kick fence to visually guide riders to understand where their position is relative to the leader. Our objective, as with all Phoenix Rising group rides, is to share some friendly banter and enjoy the camaraderie. We employ a fabulous group of sweepers to accompany and/or help riders rejoin the main yellow group. We intend that no one ride alone, unless they want to do so.

We will ride a variety of routes, mainly flatter routes, but we will occasionally do some fun climbing to add a little spice. Please feel free to suggest a route you would like to try sometime, or a badge you need that can be completed in a 60-minute time period. We're happy to add suggestions to the ride list.

For more information on all our weekly events, join our club Phoenix Rising on Zwift Companion.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit (C)
Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit (D)
Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit (E)

name: Spin and Grin
description: The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. Join us for a social ride, all abilities welcome pace will be around 1.1-1.3 wkg. During the ride try to stay with the yellow lead beacon and follow instructions from your ride leader.

name: USMeS -Systems Check (D) 1.8 - 2.2w/kg
description:
Welcome to USMeS Systems Check
This ride is designed to bring everything online… legs, cadence, breathing, and focus, before stepping into Turn It Up Tuesday immediately following. It’s a chance to settle in, find rhythm, and prepare as a group.
✅ Pace: an average of 1.8–2.2 w/kg
✅ Duration: 55 minutes
Although we aim for the ride to settle around a ~2.0 w/kg overall average, expect slight pushes on climbs, and we’ll always enjoy seeing a sprint when the moment presents itself. The priority is falling back in quickly and keeping the group together.
Ride smooth, respect the leader, and work as a unit.
Systems checked. Ready to roll.
#conqueringlimits
#challengeaccepted

eventSubgroups.name: USMeS -Systems Check (D) 1.8 - 2.2w/kg (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to the USMES Warm-up Ride.
This is one of two weekly warm-up rides, with the goal of working from this group into the next ride with a higher pace. Use this ride to help you build that solid base for your training to build on!!
These Rides are generally low key and to socialize with our members. The group set up and pace is
specifically lower to support and train for our faster groups.
Pace will start off around 1.5w/kg and could work up to 2.5w/kg on hills. With the average pace being near 2w/kg. There may be times, when traversing a hill/bump, where the pace may go higher, but overall we should be around 2w/kg.
New to our rides or to Zwift? The best place to hang-out in the ride is in the front 1/3 of the Yellow Beacons (Leaders) Peloton or Blob. This keeps you in the draft and at the advertised pace and involved with the group.
The Fence will be used if necessary to control the group.
Please don’t chase the flyers, don’t encourage them. You joined the Group ride, then ride with the group. If you are dead set on Flying, then after the ride starts, select quite the ride and you will stay on the rides route and the Fence won’t zap you! Another option is if you have the energy and legs for it, volunteer SWEEP. Help those that need a wheel back to the blob and get your Interval work in for the day!!

United States Military Endurance Sports (USMES) Zwift riders & runners, is a support group for the USMES National Club. We do not require veteran status to join the Zwift group.
USMES Zwift group Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/usmeszwiftr2
USMES National Team Facebook
Discord Channel
https://discord.com/invite/hExnM7B

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Bikealicious Sweet Spot
description:
For riders who want more than the standard 60 minute ride but 90 minutes is too much, this 80 minute ride hits the Sweet Spot in between!
We ride banded together, no one gets dropped.  All abilities are welcome. 

Course changes weekly to keep it spicy.

name: Epic Run Crew: Tuesday Trot
description:
You survived Monday! Let’s make Tuesday terrific by joining us for a friendly social run.
This is a 45 minute group run. You are welcome to run at your own pace (AYOP). Chatting is encouraged! Talk about food. Make a funny observation. Whatever. We don’t judge. Just have some fun!
Epic Run Crew is based in Vancouver, BC, Canada. We were founded as an in-person club in 2021 and then joined Zwift in June 2022.
Use the Zwift Companion app to join the Epic Run Crew club, for access to exclusive member-only group runs.
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/epicruncrew2/
Instagram: @epicruncrew

description:

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Iceni Women's Series X Femme Cycle Collab
description:
Iceni Women’s Series
X
World Tour Spring Classics 

Get ready to unleash your racing fire with the Iceni Women’s Spring Classics Series, a fierce high-intensity tribute to women in cycling, staged on Zwift and inspired by the legendary WorldTour Spring Classics. Each race mimics the demands of iconic IRL classics, from relentless tempo to all-out attacks, delivering a true test of strength, strategy, and resilience.
This series is more than just racing,  it is a tribute to women.
Held during Women’s History Month, the Iceni Women’s Spring Classics Series honours the women who paved the way in sport, the pioneers who fought for opportunity, and the riders who continue to redefine what’s possible on the world’s biggest stages. Inspired by the Iceni tribe and their fearless leader, Queen Boudicca, the series celebrates courage, unity, and the unbreakable spirit of women.
Ride the virtual roads with purpose. Embrace the grit of the classics. Whether you’re racing for the win or pushing beyond your limits, this is your moment to be part of something bigger than the result.
Join the tribe.
Ride for history.
Rebel with power.


Tour of Flanders // Challenge 5 
Watopia – Coast to Coast  ( 1 lap ) 
(https://zwiftinsider.com/route/coast-to-coast/) 
Distance: 26.7 km | Elevation: 154 m
Power-ups: AERO and Ghost 
The final race of the Iceni Women’s Spring Classics series celebrates the grit and glory of Tour of Flanders steep, punchy climbs, cobbled sections, and a race where every move counts. Set on Zwift’s Coast to Coast route, this challenge brings the spirit of Flanders’ iconic roads to life, delivering a dramatic and unforgettable finale for Women’s History Month.
Riders will tackle repeated climbs and technical sections that demand power, timing, and tactical awareness. Positioning is crucial, attacks are relentless, and the finish rewards those who can combine strength with savvy race craft. It’s a fitting tribute to the women who have shaped cycling history and continue to inspire the next generation of racers.
Steep. Strategic. Unstoppable.
The perfect way to close the series ride with purpose, honor the legacy, and finish strong.
Sprint & QOM Segments (FAL)
Sasquatch Sprint 
Woodland Sprint 
Jarvis QOM
Jarvis Sprint 
Points Scoring  : 
1st - 10 points
2nd - 8 points
3rd - 6 points
4th - 4 points
5th - 2 points
6th - 1 point 
Finishing points: 
The top five score 100, 95, 90, 86, 83, and every place after that drops by 1 point.

name: Velocious Sport Tue Night Criterium (Race)
description: Brought you by Velocious Sport Jr/u23 Racing and [DRAFT].Meet here every Tuesday at 7:15PM EST for an all out race from the start to the finish line! We'll have some Pro's race with us occasionally, so be prepared to hurt :) Race is two laps of Watopia Flat, around 20km. Categories for everyone and results separated by category. Don’t recommend TT bikes and no power ups! Choose your category based on your w/kg.

name: Ride to Conquer Cancer Training Ride
description:
All Zwifters are welcome to join the Ride to Conquer Cancer Training ride dedicated to conquering cancer.

This is a positive and inclusive ride - use it as a training or recovery ride and get social!

Like the 2-day, 200+ KM event in June every year, this is a ride, not a race. Challenge others in a sprint and then drop back with the group to socialize.
Our cycling community comes together to bring awareness to our need to accelerate cancer research. When we Ride together, we conquer cancer together.

The Ride to Conquer Cancer is Canada's largest athletic fundraiser, raising funds to support world leading cancer research - benefiting cancer patients globally.

Princess Margaret is one of the top 5 cancer research centres in the world. Learn more about Princess Margaret, the Ride to Conquer Cancer or to register / donate, visit us at ride2conquer.ca.ca and join us on Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/conquercancer

eventSubgroups.name: Ride to Conquer Cancer Training Ride (E)

name: Stage 5: Zwift Games: Three Step Sisters | Advanced
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

eventSubgroups.name: Stage 5: Zwift Games: Three Step Sisters | Advanced (A)

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Champs Elysees
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: The HERD Kick It UP!
description:
Looking for something a little more challenging than our awesome Social Rides? Join us as on our "Kick It Up for Tuesday" Group Ride.

Remember that this is NOT A RACE! Like all group rides in real life, please follow the Ride Leader's (Yellow Beacon) pace and announcements. Riders looking for a challenge are encouraged to ride with the Red Beacon and help sweep the group.

Join us on Discord during the ride https://discord.gg/Dr7ZtPV (but please use Push-To-Talk)
And check us out on Facebook for our other amazing group rides. https://www.facebook.com/groups/zHerd/

#herd

name: The Common Empire Weekly Women's Ride
description:
Join Common Empire for the women's only group ride. The
purpose of this ride is to have fun and follow a workout, provided to you
at the beginning of the ride. This ride is set as a 'keep everyone
together'. Please follow the leaders instructions to make it pleasant for
all the participants.
#jointheride

name: Turn It Up Tuesdays
description:
Welcome to Turn It Up Tuesdays. Whether you’re a new runner getting into fitness, an experienced runner who wants to compete for a new personal best, or an athlete craving a great workout alongside an awesome crew of fellow athletes, these weekly structured workouts promise up-tempo runs that’ll boost you up and help you achieve your running goals. 

#turnituptuesday #tiut

name: Good Time Riders - TT Race
description:
Test yourself over a different 10-mile TT route each week.

Good Time Riders are a group of online friends who offer range of weekly
group rides, workouts and races, where enjoyment is key. Find us on Zwift
Clubs, Discord and Facebook

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: USMeS - Turn it Up Tuesday Early [C] 2.2 - 2.8w/kg
description:
Turn It Up Tuesday Early 2.2-2.8
Now that you’re Warmed-Up, Time to Turn Up the Intensity!

Ready to dial up the watts? Turn It Up Tuesday Early is a steady C-paced ride designed to push your endurance, sharpen your legs, and build strength for the week ahead.

Pace: 2.2–2.8 w/kg 
Average: ~2.5 w/kg 
Fence: In use to keep the group together—we’ll drop it for sprints, but regroup quickly

Ride Format:
    • Steady, consistent pacing
    • Sprint segments announced in advance—chase your PRs!
    • Regroup behind the beacon after each effort
    • Friendly, focused vibe to help you stay motivated

Expectations:
 Hold a smooth tempo between sprints
 Respect the fence when active—keep the pack tight
 Regroup promptly after any all-out sections

This is your chance to turn up the effort. Perfect for building fitness. Come ready to ride steady, sprint hard, and regroup fast.

Please join our Discord https://discord.gg/uyE2eUh4yE  and join our Zwift Club and FB by searching “US Military eSports”

 #conqueringlimits   #challengeaccepted

eventSubgroups.name: USMeS - Turn it Up Tuesday Early [C] 2.2 - 2.8w/kg (C)

name: Mountain Massif Group Run
description:
Join Mountain Massif for some relaxed group runs. The goal is to simply complete the route and collect the badge.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Film My Run 500 with NoblePro
description:
Join Stephen Cousins and NoblePro for a 500 metre climb. Set your treadmill to 12% incline and run 4.2km of distance to reach 500 metres elevation. If your treadmill doesn't go that high, set it as high as it will go and join us anyway!

The run will be progressive starting at a walking pace of 5kph rising to 7kph for the final kilometre. Get on to the balls of your toes and get your calf muscles to do the work. This continuous uphill effort will help build strength and endurance for those hilly trail runs

This event is sponsored by the ever popular Noble Pro treadmills. You can get a discount on a NoblePro SMART treadmill from this link https://zwift.run/noble-pro

You can watch the run on the live stream at youtube.com/ZwiftRunChannel

Join the FMR Run Club for more fun events at https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/6afca04c-8fac-4ffa-b700-f27a73d7f3dd/join

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: ZLDR AYOP Event
description:
Start your day on the right foot or the left. Come to our 5k at your own pace (AYOP). Run, walk, chat, laugh and get your day moving on a positive note! Attitude is 90% of any situation so let’s make this day the best day and get you in the best mindset so you can be the best you!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn
ZLDR website for Challenge Months: https://www.zldr.club
Online shop: https://www.sweatydino.com

name: A&B Cycle - Group Training Ride
description: Spirited group training ride on the Watopia course will average 2.5-3.1w/kg we will participate in sprints and kom's which will increase the intensity

name: Dutch Diesel Tempo Tuesday
description:
A group ride at a steady pace between 2.2 - 3.0 w/kg. This is a group ride, not a race , but it is also a drop ride, so if you get dropped you'll need to catch up! Come join us for some muscular endurance training. The pace of the ride will be in the C category, but we welcome riders of all categories to ride and train with us. If you like to chat during the ride you can join our discord channel.

If you’d like to join the ride leader for a chat while riding, you can join our discord channel https://discord.gg/vUtZNsr.  For our complete ride schedule visit https://www.dutchdieselcycling.com/rideschedule

#ddc

name: Bicycle Way of Life Mechanics' Cruiseday Ride
description:
You are invited to join us for a ride with us bike people after a day's work at the bike shops!

We always plan to provide lead and sweep support, but there will be times where that will not be the case especially during summertime where a rubberbanding mode will be turned on.

You are invited to join us for a ride with us bike people after a day's work at the bike shops!
Want to know more about us? Bicycle Way of Life (bicycleway.com) is a local bike store chain in Eugene, Oregon. We encourage new and experienced riders to support each other indoors and out, fixing trails or just their bikes.

Interact and find more info. by joining our FB group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bwolzwift
Look us up on Strava https://www.strava.com/clubs/bicycleway-com, Instagram https://www.instagram.com/bicyclewayoflife/, and Facebook https://www.facebook.com/bicycleway/

eventSubgroups.name: Bicycle Way of Life Mechanics' Cruiseday Ride (D)

name: RCC Global Club Ride
description:
WELCOME TO THE RAPHA CYCLING CLUB WORLD WIDE GROUP RIDE. 
 
This is a social group workout; a chance every week for members to get together from around the globe, ride, chat and support each other - it’s run as a group workout session with ‘keep everyone together’ enabled so no matter what your pace you’ll stay in the pack - it’s not a race or competition and open for anyone to join us. 
We host 4 rides every week on a Tuesday to suit different time zones around the world (aimed at evenings), join whichever time works best for you. 
 
WHO IS THE RCC?
 
The Rapha Cycling Club (RCC) is, as the name suggests, Rapha’s Cycling Club. The RCC exists to help connect like minded riders from all over the world, underpinned by Rapha’s products and experiences. We ride together across continents and here united within Zwift. 
 
For more information head to: www.Rapha.cc/rcc
 
RIDE LEADERS
 
The rides are led by our friendly team of member ride leaders from around the world.  The Leader (who has a yellow beacon over their head) will ride at a steady pace as set by the workout session. Prompts will appear on screen set by the workout designer Matt Rowe so you know what to do throughout the ride. On most occasions we will have a support ride leader (who will also have a beacon), the ride leaders are there to help support you and keep the banter going - to join the chat use the Zwift Companion smartphone app. 
 
ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS
 
Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.
 
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

Today's workout contains 4 x 6 minute blocks of Zone 3, with some surges thrown in for good measure! The Zone 3 riding should feel nice and comfortable, however the surges may sting a little. The surges in each block are only 20 seconds long, but at 110% of FTP so require a focused increase in effort .At the end of each 6 minute block, there is a 1 minute 'Free Ride' - this is your chance to turn an endurance based session into something special! Come event day or when riding with friends, it's likely you want some more power for that final sprint to the cafe right? Give it 100% in each 60 second Free Ride section to help you become more explosive! Stress points 45: #Social
 
 
WORKOUT DESIGNER
 
Matt Rowe is the founder of Rowe & King Coaching (www.roweandking.com) with 25 years of cycling experience, coaching amateur cyclists all over the world to  achieve their personal goals.
 
HELPFUL HINT
 
We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level.
You can adjust the difficulty of the workout mid-ride if needed, by using the up/down arrows on the left hand side, under the workout details.
 
HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#rcc

name: Intermountain Healthcare Tuesday Group Ride
description:
Team Intermountain was originally founded 11 years ago by 5 friends that rode together. The camaraderie they built while riding helped each of them to further develop their fitness and overall cycling ability. They felt like other cycling clubs only existed for the benefit of a few; rather than existing for all its members. Each of the original founders balanced family, career, and cycling. So, they understood the importance of involving those who support the cyclist in these activities.

Today these philosophies continue to guide the club. In the last 11 years, the club has grown to become one of the largest in the state of Utah. Now, we're taking to Zwift and expanding our reach to everyone!

IMH hosts a Tuesday night ride all spring and summer at 6PM leaving from Ogden, Utah and now anyone is welcome to join over the colder months. The groups will be similar to those that exist outside. 

Please join our Discord to chat with fellow riders during the ride. https://discord.gg/Nfx82RmHvm

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Champs Elysees
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER?

name: Bikealicious Tuesday Tango
description:
We have two categories to choose from C and E. If you choose C you will be in a non-banded paced ride averaging 2.5-3.2 w/kg. You can also choose category E a rubberband ride where everyone works and stays together (a no drop ride).

Open conversation is a must, but if you're the shy one it's okay.
Dad jokes are encouraged.

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3

Date: March 30 - April 5

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

name: bet-at-home Midweek Ride
description: "Celebrate" midweek with this group ride. Its ideal for riders who want a solide ride in a bunch. We will ride around 2.5-3.0 W/kg, but will adjust this to the group as necessary. Please stay together as a group. Do not forget, its a steady ride and not a race. If you want to race, then please join our other events. Everyone is welcome, as long as you want.

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: USMeS - Turn it Up Tuesday Late [C] 2.2 - 2.8w/kg
description:
Turn It Up Tuesday Late 2.2-2.8
Late Ride…Time to Turn Up the Intensity!

Ready to dial up the watts after a long day? Turn It Up Tuesday Late is a steady C-paced ride designed to push your endurance, sharpen your legs, and build strength for the week ahead.

Pace: 2.2–2.8 w/kg 
Average: ~2.5 w/kg overall
Fence: In use to keep the group together—we’ll drop it for sprints, but regroup quickly

Ride Format:

    • Smooth, consistent pacing with no big surges between sprints
    • Sprint segments announced in advance—chase your PRs!
    • Regroup behind the beacon after each effort
    • Friendly, focused vibe to help you stay motivated

Expectations:
 Hold a steady tempo between sprints
 Respect the fence when active—keep the pack tight
 Regroup promptly after any all-out sections

This is your chance to turn up the effort. Perfect for building fitness and getting stronger every week.

Come ready to ride steady, sprint hard, and regroup fast.

Please join our Discord https://discord.gg/uyE2eUh4yE  and join our Zwift Club and FB by searching “US Military eSports”

#conqueringlimits    #challengeaccepted

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!
