name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

rolling out of Watopia at 6 pm Thursdays.

It’s a tempo training ride – of course, you are welcome to go as hard as
you like.

Visit www.bicyclingaustralia.com.au for the latest cycling-related news in
Australia.

Join, share & spread the word using the #BowralClassic hashtag or visit

name: ZLDR Run Like No One is Watching
description:
Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a 30 minute run. Whether you want to push for a new personal best, use it as part of your weekly training program or just enjoy a sociable run this is suitable for you. Come and do an easy walk or a quick sprint, whatever suits your training for the day!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

#zldr_run

The EVR Race is intended to provide an entry level race on Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays for A, B, C and D racers in four different timezones. All categories start together. You are encouraged to race with the fastest participants you can keep up with. 

Be prepared for fast starts and hard racing.

Results can be seen at zwiftpower.com and will be based on your category.

name: Righto Champ Riot p/b Team VTO
description:
Join Team VTO’s Ben Shepherd (shep_just_shep) and Ben Pratt (the_average_ironman) for a legendary 5km run in partnership with Righto Champ Sunglasses—because every champ deserves to look spec-tacular! 🕶️🏃

This isn’t just any run—it’s a chance to celebrate men’s mental health, share laughs, and break a sweat. Whether you’re fast, slow, or somewhere in between, this event is all about fun and connection. 🎉

🎁 Bonus: A few lucky runners will score stylish Righto Champ Sunglasses!

Lace up, bring your best dad jokes, and let’s run, laugh, and champion mental health together.

Why did the dad bring a ladder to the race?
Because he wanted to go to the next level! 😄

See you there—because nothing says self-care like running 5km and having a laugh! 💪👏

Team Zwift Tri Oz is the home of Multisport Athletes in Australia on @gozwift.

We value FUN , FRIENDSHIP & DETERMINATION !

For more information, please join our Zwift Club - Team VTO on Zwift Companion
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/151ca95c-f6de-4fe2-ae88-a769e367f439/home

Join the chat on FB;
https://www.facebook.com/groups/thenewzwifttrioz

name: Stage 5: Zwift Games: Three Step Sisters
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: Workout Wednesday Run
description:
Join the Zwift Runners for a group run workout that will be of benefit to all distance runners.

The speeds for each group are based on a threshold speed of A group 15 kph, B 13 kph, C 11 kph, D 9 kph. We will start the intervals at that speed but increase the speed of the intervals we get into the workout. We encourage runners to choose the group closest to the effort level you want to run at and to keep to the nominal speed for that group to maintain the group dynamic. However if these speeds are significantly too low or high for you then you can choose to run in the group that is closest and then adjust the target speed up to 10% up or down in the game or via the Companion app.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Running in general then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook group or zwift.com/run #gozwiftrun #wow

name: ZLDR Decaf Wednesday's
description:
We will ride for 45min with no coffee stop (Decaf) 

Yellow beacon will aim for average 1.8 wkg.
Red beacon will aim for average 1.6 wkg. 
Feel free to ride with Yellow or Red. 

All rider's are encouraged to ride within the advertised pace and stick with the ride leader. 

Ride On!

name: AusCycling Talent Identification
description:
Your journey to the top step starts in your living room.

AusCycling invites you to test yourself against the best. The workout comprises three free ride efforts: 15 seconds, 3 minutes and 12 minutes designed to test your critical power. As with any test or race, ensure you are appropriately warmed up and motivated.

The test is short and sharp, requiring maximum commitment. Erg mode will be turned off during the intervals, you are on your own and its your time to shine.

As you fade through the efforts, use your gears to reduce the resistance and maintain your cadence. Aiming for 90-100rpm for the efforts. Finish the efforts completely spent, the key is to finish all the efforts regardless of how much your power fades or how hard it feels.

Saddle up and enjoy the ride!

 Target Audience;
 16-22yo with no high-performance sport background
 People with vision or physical impairment – any age

People with background in high-performance sport – any age
*If you are outside the ages of 16-22 and have a background in high performance sport or vision/physical impairment please visit https://www.auscycling.org.au/page/virtual-tid and contact us for more information.

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Coach Frank's Training Series
description:
B Group pace: 3-4w/kg

We’ll use the terrain and group dynamics for intensity

Get on the Discord channel to talk training

name: Restart Esports - Wednesday Worlds
description:
Restart Esports - instagram.com/restart.esports

1. Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) is mandatory for all participants.

2. Power Source is Mandatory for all racers. A smart trainer or smart bike should be your power source where possible. ZPower is not allowed. 

3. Dual Recording - Should always be done if you have the capability. All podium positions are expected to dual record or have at least have a recent dual recording history on their profile. Any rider posting high level performances is expected to validate their hardware with dual recordings as a minimum. 

Results WILL be amended (if required), in the interest of fair play, for all Restart Esports hosted events.

name: Chat and roll p/b TBR
description:
Join the TBR crew and roll with Chris Hoiberg for a 45min 2.2 - 2.3 w/kg cruise and chat. Our yellow beacon will average around 40km/h (25mph), consistent with a category C flat ride, with a bit of variation to cater for the majority of riders. Our red beacon may create a second group in the last half of the ride and average around 38km/h (24mph) to help as many riders as possible to finish in a group.

The aim of the ride is to support those wanting to progress from sub 2w/kg rides to something a bit quicker as they improve their strength and fitness. Stronger riders are welcome and your assistance in the group will be appreciated as we will have sweepers working throughout the ride.

So come along at 8.15am AEST each Wednesday and see what makes TBR such a friendly bunch. You're all welcome and you'll have a mighty good time!

We don't use the fence for this ride. All riders should exercise self-control and respect the ride leader by riding with the yellow beacon at all times. Your co-operation helps us provide everyone with a fun, supportive and inclusive ride.

Follow TBR at thebigring.cc to learn more about our events, read ride reports and leave feedback.

name: Stage 1: Zwift Games: Kaze Kicker | Advanced
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: A Face full of Gravel Beginners series
description:
Old Dog Racing presents a series of short scenic gravel races ideal for beginner and intermediate riders. 

Perhaps you are new; maybe the old power station isn’t delivering the watts anymore, or the knees are just a little too creaky!

Come and join the fun in six weekly races, two start times, Wednesdays 5:30 pm Eastern and  Fridays 5:30 pm Pacific, If you race both times in a week your best finish will go towards your points total.

The Closely spaced non-standard categories are: 
A 3.4 - 4.0 W/kg;  B 3.0 - 3.39 W/kg; C 2.3 - 2.99 W/kg; D 1.8-2.29 W/kg and E 0-1.79 W/kg

All riders will be provided with a Specialized Crux Gravel bike and visible to each other with a staggered start…Category E will speed off first and subsequent categories as follows; D 2 minutes later; C and B a minute Later each and Finally, A  riders 30 seconds after that. (intervals may vary slightly between events depending on expected completion times.).

Riders will be issued the Zwift Category kit as an aid to identify which category riders you are currently racing against. Try and hang on to passing riders for as long as possible, stretch yourself!

Power ups to help you along your way include: The van, the steamroller and the feather
If you find yourself alone, enjoy the scenery and clock up another route badge.

Points awarded to each category in every race are: 25, 20,16,13,11,10,9,8,7,6 and 5 very generous participation points!! (You have to finish!). 
Highest accumulated points in each category wins the series.. you’re going to have to wear an HRM and have a Zwift Power account in order to be awarded points.

Earn more than 100 points and l’ll send you an Old Dog Racing Mug!*
The best race report of the series gets a mug too.* 
      *terms and conditions apply, go to Facebook page for details.

Join us on :
Zwift Companion Club - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/0ad7655f-797b-4dc2-940d-b313fca0df0a/home
Facebook -    https://www.facebook.com/groups/3001328763388965 
or Strava  -  https://strava.app.link/7JqgUWGwu1b

WOOF!

name: 3R Endurance Steady Ride
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. 

This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ 

#team3r #ride3r
#rideraceraise.

#3r

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: PartyAnimal 80 (Wheelsky 3.5+)
description:
Meet the most notorious zwifter group: Party Animal 80. Hop on. Grab the wheels. You are welcome:)

We won't call out ""front easy"" or ""back push push"". Use your zwift etiquette.

B++ 80 minutes of various party forms (unexpected race pace, strava segment PR breaker, chase the ace, TTT, silent auction, birthday celebration, anything you can reinvent out of zwift box).

When we realize we can never be one of those pros, we party in Zwiftopia. But hey, It's ok to be the minority. Do you train hard or hardly train? Or, you just wanna look tough? Join us. Be there or be dry.

#PA80

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt17 RUN
description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: Giant Wednesday Motivation ride series
description:
Are you looking for the perfect social ride after an epic weekend of riding, or maybe just looking for some motivation to get the week going?

Look no further, the #GiantWednesdayMotivation ride is here for you to kick off the week with your fellow Zwifters, along with live Q&A in the chat. Our ride leaders will do their best to keep the group together with a 2.5-2.7 w/kg effort.

Use #GiantWednesdayMotivation and tag @GiantBicyclesUSA on social media to let us know you're joining the ride!

#wednesdaymotivation

name: SWX Wednesday Tempo by Coach Cindy 🇺🇸🇨🇦🇧🇷
description:
Build strength and control every Wednesday with SWX Tempo — a steady endurance builder designed to grow your aerobic engine.
Crafted by Coach Cindy (Train Smart = Race Stronger), this session focuses on sustained tempo efforts to improve muscular endurance, pacing, and long-ride efficiency. Bring smooth cadence, mental focus, and your best zone 3 vibes!

• All levels welcome
• Stay-together mode enabled
• Late join allowed

Connect and learn more about who we are:
📸 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/shark_worx_swx
🌐 Website: https://bit.ly/Visit_Coach_Cindy

name: Ascenders Asia Morning Group Run
description:
Come and join the Ascenders as we put on our running shoes and run
daily with our friends too by starting our day in a healthy way. Research
shows that just 30 minutes of running can have huge benefits on your
short-term and long-term health. So we designed our running events to
motivate athletes to run 30 minutes each day to boost your metabolism and
use those calories. Running will also help you look good and feel stronger.

Our group run is designed with runners of all levels in mind and set the
pace range to be determined by our Zwifter Runners. So find a group pace
that matches how you feel each day and make those steps count for you.
Runners who went beyond the event duration will find that we have selected
courses that will help you earn the Route Badges when you run the full
distance.

So lace up those running shoes as everybody is welcome to join with our
friendly and motivating runners.

# <https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/> Join Ascenders on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# <https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/> Ascenders Discord Channel:
https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: USAT Workout Wednesday
description: This USA Triathlon ride will work on speed, power and strength. The ride contains a series of short, upper threshold segments followed by a challenging low cadence build to simulate climbing. The workout ends with descending efforts while increasing cadence. This USAT workout was prepared by Chris Kaplanis, professional coach and co-founder of RTA Triathlon. www.RTAtri.com | Social: @RTAtriathlon

name: Safe Roads with It Could Be Me
description:
We want to invite all interested in cycling safety and getting involved in their communities to join us.
All abilities are welcomed.
We want to strengthen our It Could Be Me community to help eradicate the current negative narrative between drivers and vulnerable users.

https://www.itcouldbeme.org

Join us on Discord for articles, updates and, community events - including our interactive group rides on Zwift. Follow the link and help us make roads safer together.

https://discord.gg/zMtetyx3P9

name: Ravecovery
description:
Welcome to Team Rave

It’s time to heal those dancing injuries from all the raving or the sore muscles from all the racing.   Join Team RAVE for a recovery ride with the Rave USA crew.  This is not a race but feel free to challenge yourself or take it easy.

The yellow beacon will cruise at 1.8 w/kg - 2w/kg. The red beacon at 1.0 w/kg .
Find a group that suits you, blob up but above all, HAVE FUN! That’s all that matters
#teamrave - rave like there's no tomorrow!

name: Team I Race Like A Girl Workout
description:
We are in our build phase for a few weeks. Intensity will be added progressively with a variety of workouts targeting top end power. Our favorite high intensity intervals will also continue to make a regular comeback. Use these workouts as a smart addition to your weekly training plans!

ZWIFT WORKOUTS

We have two rides scheduled each week on Wednesdays: 6:05 am EST (with ride leader) and 6:45 pm EST (without ride leader).

Want to join more IRLAG Rides and Runs? Check out our upcoming socials rides through our I Race Like a Girl club feature.

ABOUT ANGELA NAETH & I RACE LIKE A GIRL

Angela Naeth is a professional triathlete, coach, and founder of IRACELIKEAGIRL - a community of girls/women who provide opportunities for females of all ages and abilities to support, encourage and inspire one another in the sport of triathlon, cycling, running and swimming. She's raced for over 10 years from Sprint to Ironman and has coached triathletes/runners/cyclists for the last 7 years. https://www.iracelikeagirl.com/

IRACELIKEAGIRL and GIRLSGETGRITTY team: https://www.iracelikeagirl.com/
E-racing team: https://www.facebook.com/groups/1790418584457764/
IRLAG podcast: https://open.spotify.com/show/4IwS1l9E8S6AzzhFryTxol?si=74a0ddb595fe4ce6

name: Group Run 5k
description:
DESCRIPTION

Join us for a 5k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends.

name: The HERD Wacky Wednesday
description:
Whoop whoop, it's Wednesday! Come join The Herd on a silly and social roll around Zwift's finest routes!

This ride is a great choice for beginner and intermediate riders, or anyone looking for a recovery. Expect a LOT of fun chat, games, and a few surprises.

We focus on sticking as a group, and honouring the advertised pace of 1.3-1.5 w/kg (about 105W). Please stay with the Yellow Beacon. Stronger riders are encouraged to help the Red Beacon sweep anyone struggling off the back.

For even more fun, connect to our Discord voice channel, where you can join the chat or just listen in: see www.bit.ly/HerdDiscord to set yourself up before the ride. And check out The Herd’s Facebook group and many other goodies at www.TheHerd.Club.

#herd

name: ZTBR Endurance Group Ride (E)
description:
ENGLISH:
ZTBR eCycling holds this easy-paced ride every Wednesday! ALL ARE WELCOME!

Audience: The tour is suitable for new riders, those recovering from injury or anyone looking for a fun and regenerative experience.
Pace: This tour focuses on 1.0-2.5 w/kg. Please respect the leader's (who will give directions in the Chat), pace and walk along for better dynamics. Groups at a slower pace than the leader can form,

Stronger riders are welcome to help sweep or act as riding companions for others. If you're having trouble, send a message in Chat to ask for help from the ride scan.

We have late ticket enabled. If you fall off early on, it might be easier to just get off the ride and get back on. Late join works for the first 30 minutes of the trip.

This tour is normally 90 minutes long, but sometimes it can be longer or shorter to cover a specific Zwift route.

Join us on Instagram @ztbr_ecycling [website] ztbr.com.br

#ztbr #z2

==============================================

PORTUGUÊS:
O ZTBR eCycling realizada todas as quartas-feiras este passeio de ritmo leve! TODOS SÃO BEM-VINDOS!

Público: O passeio é adequado para novos pilotos, aqueles que se recuperam de uma lesão ou qualquer pessoa que busca uma experiência divertida e regenerativa.
Ritmo: Este passeio tem como foco 1,0-2,5 w/kg. Por favor, respeite o ritmo do líder (que passará orientações no Chat), e ande junto para melhor dinâmica. Grupos em ritmo menor que o líder poderão se formar, 

Os pilotos mais fortes são bem-vindos para ajudar na varredura ou atuar como companheiros de passeio para os outros. Se você estiver com dificuldades, envie uma mensagem no Chat para solicitar ajuda da varredura de passeio.

Nós temos o ingresso tardio ativado. Se você cair no início, pode ser mais fácil simplesmente sair do passeio e voltar. Late join funciona nos primeiros 30 minutos da viagem.

Esse passeio normalmente dura 90 minutos, mas às vezes pode ser mais longo ou mais curto para cobrir uma rota específica do Zwift.

Junte-se a nós no Instagran @ztbr_ecycling [website] ztbr.com.br

#ztbr #z2

name: Rhino Banded Hot Dog Social
description:
Rhino Hot Dog Social ride is the epitome of the traditional Wednesday night club run.
The mid week recovery ride to punctuate the races that bookend the week. A purely social ride that aims to cater for everyone, ride is banded so whatever your pace you will stay with the yellow beacon leader group. So come and join Rhino Racing for a virtual Hot Dog, enjoy the company and have a fun Wednesday ride out. It's all about the ride!!

name: Bikealicious Wednesday Worlds
description:
Originating with a group a Zwifters located near Boston, Massachusetts, Wednesday Worlds is a banded/stay together ride open to cyclists and triathletes of all abilities, with novice/intermediate/advanced participants especially welcome. As a stay together (Rubberband) ride, you never will get dropped, ( unless you stop pedaling) thanks to Zwift's banded group ride feature. Ride as hard or as easy as you like, without worry of disrupting the overall ride. The goal of this ride is to provide a supportive opportunity for Zwifters to get together to ride and (if desired) socialize. Courses will vary each week and each ride will run 60 or 90 minutes. If you are looking for a consistent weekly ride with all-around enthusiastic people who are interested in all aspects of cycling, this event might be for you!


Your ride leader has a 40+ year history of top level cycling competition on both the road and track. Additionally, he draws on many years of coaching regional and national level cyclists and his work on aerodynamic testing to provide additional support to ride participants. Questions during the ride are encouraged!

name: Major Taylor 1899 (MT1899) Whirlwind Wednesday
description:
Join us for the Major Taylor 1899 Whirlwind Wednesday!

The ride leader will hold ~2.0 w/kg on the flats for the first 50 minutes to keep the group together. In the final 10 minutes, the fence drops, time to PUSH THE PACE and see who can hang on! 

This is a non-banded ride, so maintaining the stated pace is key to staying with the beacon. Please respect group ride etiquette.

Why Major Taylor?

Marshall Walter Taylor, known as “Major Taylor”, was a trailblazing cycling legend. In 1899, he became the first U.S. born African American world champion in any sport, wining the 1-mile and 2-mile sprint world titles, along with multiple national championships. 

This ride is hosted by the Major Taylor 1899 (MT1899) community cycling clubs across the USA united to honor his legacy. The acronym after each ride leader’s name identifies the hosting club.

Want to ride IRL with a local Major Taylor club?
https://www.majortaylorassociation.org/friends.shtml

Fun FACT: One of Major Taylor’s nicknames was “The Worcester Whirlwind.”

During the months of May through October there may be rides without an assigned ride leader due to the outdoor riding season. We invite all to connect with us on the social media platforms below to stay in the know:

Discord
https://discord.gg/PwT4BgU7au

Facebook
https://www.facebook.com/groups/418937925806730/?ref=share

Strava
https://www.strava.com/clubs/mt1899

Zwift Power
https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=12949

As we say in the Zwift community, Ride On!
